column1: Notes on Duplications and Expansion:
Selected Key Source Texts and Quotes
Euripides, The Bacchae (c. 405 BCE):
“I am Dionysus, son of Zeus, come to Thebes to establish my mysteries.” (line 64)
Source: Euripides, The Bacchae, trans. E.H. Plumptre, 19th c.
Herodotus, Histories (5th c. BCE):
“The Thracians say that Zalmoxis is a god.” (4.93)
Source: Herodotus, Histories, trans. A.D. Godley, Loeb Classical Library.
John 1:1-3, 18 (New Testament):
“In the beginning was the Word (Logos), and the Word was with God, and the Word was God... No one has ever seen God; the only Son... who is in the bosom of the Father.”
Source: The Holy Bible, John 1:1-3, 18.
Justin Martyr, Apology (c. 150 CE):
“Christ is the true Dionysus, who suffered and rose again.” (1.26)
Source: Justin Martyr, First Apology, trans. Marcus Dods.
Pliny the Younger, Letters (c. 100 CE):
“At Andros, the wine jar is miraculously filled every year on the 5th of January.” (10.96)
Source: Pliny the Younger, Epistulae, trans. Betty Radice.
Cicero, De Natura Deorum (45 BCE):
“Sabazius, the son of the Great Mother Cybele, is the Phrygian Dionysus.” (3.22)
Source: Cicero, On the Nature of the Gods, trans. H. Rackham.
Livy, Ab Urbe Condita (c. 27 BCE):
“The Senate issued a decree banning Bacchanalia due to their secret, disorderly rites.” (39.8)
Source: Livy, History of Rome, trans. B.O. Foster.
